content: TURTLE SYSTEM 1 (S1) — Entry: Buy when price exceeds 20-day Donchian channel high. Sell short when price drops below 20-day low. Exit: 10-day Donchian breakout in opposite direction. Filter: Skip S1 breakout if last S1 trade was profitable (range-bound market likely). Exception: if skipped signal triggers AND market makes new 55-day extreme, take S2 entry instead. Stop: 2×ATR(20) from entry price. Position sizing: (Account × 1%) / (ATR(20) × point value). Pyramiding: Add at +0.5ATR intervals, max 4 units. Move earlier stops to breakeven after 2nd unit. CRYPTO: Use 10-day entry/5-day exit for BTC (faster trends). Use 20-day/10-day for ETH and large caps. Apply to 4H candles for intraday crypto.

TURTLE SYSTEM 2 (S2) — Entry: Buy when price exceeds 55-day Donchian channel high. Sell short on new 55-day low. Exit: 20-day Donchian breakout in opposite direction. No filter—take every S2 signal. S2 was the core system for most Turtles. Longer lookback = fewer whipsaws, bigger moves, higher win rate. Stop: 2×ATR(20). Total max risk per market: 4 units × 2ATR = 8ATR = 2% of account. CRYPTO: Use 30-day entry/15-day exit for BTC. S2 captures major crypto cycle trends (3-12 month moves).

TURTLE POSITION SIZING — N = ATR(20). Position unit = (Account × 1%) / (N × Dollar per Point). Example: $100K account, BTC ATR = $2,000. Unit = $1,000 / $2,000 = 0.5 BTC per unit. Normalizes risk across all assets. Max 4 units per market. Max 12 units per correlated group. Max 10 units in one direction. Max 20 units total. CRYPTO: With 24/7 markets, recalculate ATR using 24-hour periods, not trading sessions.

TURTLE PYRAMIDING — Add to winning positions in 0.5ATR increments. Entry = Unit 1. +0.5ATR = Unit 2. +1.0ATR = Unit 3. +1.5ATR = Unit 4. Each unit stop = 2ATR from ITS entry. After Unit 2, move Unit 1 stop to breakeven. Total max risk per market = 2% of account. CRYPTO: In strong BTC trends, pyramiding turned 2% positions into 8% positions capturing 300%+ moves. Only pyramid winners. NEVER add to losers.

TURTLE PORTFOLIO RISK LIMITS — Max 4 units per market. Max 10 units in highly correlated markets (all BTC-correlated). Max 12 units per sector (all DeFi). Max 20 units total. CRYPTO: Treat BTC+ETH+SOL+AVAX as ONE correlated group (>0.8 correlation). During crashes, crypto correlations → 1.0. True diversification requires non-crypto assets. Within crypto, diversify across sectors: L1s, DeFi, meme, infrastructure.

ELDER'S TRIPLE SCREEN — Screen 1 (weekly): MACD-Histogram determines strategic direction. Rising = long only. Falling = short only. Screen 2 (daily): Force Index 2-EMA or Stochastic identifies countertrend entry—buy dips in uptrend, sell rallies in downtrend. Screen 3 (intraday): Breakout for precision entry. Factor of Five links timeframes (each ~5× lower). Rules: (1) Never trade against weekly trend. (2) Wait for daily oscillator pullback. (3) Enter on intraday confirmation. (4) Stop at recent swing extreme. (5) Risk ≤2% per trade. CRYPTO: Use weekly/daily/4H. Weekly MACD on BTC determines alt direction too.

2% RULE + 6% RULE — 2% Rule: Never risk more than 2% of account equity on any single trade. Calculate: Equity × 2% = max dollar risk. Divide by risk per share/coin = max position. 6% Rule: If monthly equity drops 6% below last month's close, STOP ALL TRADING for remainder of month. Close all positions, go to sidelines. Total open risk cannot exceed 6% (3 trades × 2%). CRYPTO: Use 1% per trade (higher volatility). Use daily 3% stop-loss rule instead of monthly 6% (crypto moves happen in hours, not weeks).

CIRCUIT BREAKER SYSTEM — Day 1: Down 2% in single day → all position sizes CUT IN HALF. Day 2: Down 3% in single day → CLOSE EVERYTHING. Week 1: Down 5% in a week → half position sizes. Peak-to-trough: Down 10% from peak → bot STOPS completely, writes BLOCK FILE. Block file is deliberate—must manually investigate, understand why, and consciously delete to resume. CRYPTO: Add exchange-specific circuit breakers. If any exchange goes down, have fallback execution path. If stablecoin depegs >2%, exit all positions immediately.

KELLY CRITERION — f* = (p × b - q) / b, where p = win probability, b = win/loss ratio, q = 1-p. PRACTICAL: Use HALF-Kelly (f*/2) for safety. Example: 40% win rate, 3:1 R/R → f* = 0.2 (20%). Half-Kelly = 10%. Never exceed full Kelly. Kelly assumes Gaussian returns—fails during fat-tail events. CRYPTO: Use quarter-Kelly due to fat tails and regime instability. If you can't calculate Kelly accurately (>100 trades needed), use fixed 1% risk per trade.

THREE-COMPONENT EXIT — (1) Protective stop: 2-3×ATR(14) from entry. Never widen. (2) Trailing stop: Chandelier exit = Highest High - 3×ATR(22). Locks in profits as trend extends. (3) Time stop: Exit after N bars if no profit (20 bars swing, 50 bars position). Take partial profits at 1:1 R/R (move stop to breakeven), 2:1 R/R (take 1/3), trail rest. CRYPTO: Use 2×ATR stops (tighter due to 24/7 vol). Set OCO orders (stop-loss + take-profit) simultaneously to automate exits.

DONCHIAN TREND WITH EMA FILTER — Entry: 20-day Donchian channel breakout. Exit: 10-day breakout in opposite direction. Trend filter: Only take longs when 25-EMA > 350-EMA, shorts when 25-EMA < 350-EMA. Trend filter dramatically improves edge ratio by filtering counter-trend false breakouts. E-ratio improves from 1.20 to 1.35+. CRYPTO: Use 10/200 EMA on daily for BTC. Use 13/50 EMA on 4H for altcoin swing trades.

DUAL MOVING AVERAGE SYSTEM — Buy when 100-day MA crosses above 350-day MA. Sell when it crosses below. Always in market. No stops needed (the crossover IS the exit). Tested: CAGR 57.8%, MAR 1.82 over 10 years. CRYPTO: Use 20-week/50-week SMA on BTC weekly for major cycle direction. Use 50-day/200-day EMA on daily for swing trading. Golden cross (50 > 200) = bull market. Death cross = bear market.

CROSS-SECTIONAL MOMENTUM — Rank all instruments by past N-month return. Buy top decile, sell bottom decile. Rebalance monthly. Long-short portfolio is market neutral. Best lookback: 12 months for equities, 3-6 months for crypto. Key driver: forced asset sales (liquidations, index rebalancing) create persistent momentum. CRYPTO: Rank top 50 crypto by 90-day return. Long top 10, avoid bottom 10. Rebalance monthly.

MEAN REVERSION DETECTION — ADF test: p-value < 0.05 = stationary (mean-reverting). Hurst exponent: H < 0.5 = mean reverting, H = 0.5 = random walk, H > 0.5 = trending. Half-life: Regress dP on P(t-1), half-life = -ln(2)/lambda. Sweet spot: 3-20 days. Trade: When z-score exceeds ±2, enter mean reversion. Long when z < -2, short when z > +2. Exit at z = 0. CRYPTO: ETH/BTC ratio shows mean reversion. Use 30-day lookback for z-score. Beware regime changes.

TIME SERIES MOMENTUM — Buy instruments with positive N-month return, sell those with negative. Hold for 1 month. Best lookback: 12 months for equities, 3-6 months for crypto. CRYPTO: Buy BTC when 3-month return positive, sell/short when negative. Combine with volatility filter: reduce size when 30-day realized vol > 80% annualized.

KALMAN FILTER PAIRS TRADING — Dynamically estimates hedge ratio, adapting to changing relationships. Superior to static regression. Entry: When z-score exceeds ±2. Exit: z = 0. CRYPTO: Trade BTC/ETH spread, SOL/AVAX spread. Use 30-day rolling window for cointegration test. If half-life > 60 days, don't trade. If cointegration breaks (p > 0.10), exit immediately regardless of P&L.

FORCE INDEX — Formula: Price Change × Volume. 2-day EMA for entries, 13-day EMA for trend. BUY: 2-day FI dips to new low in uptrend (selling climax). SELL: 2-day FI rallies to new high in downtrend. 13-day EMA above zero = bulls control. Divergences give strong reversal signals. CRYPTO: Adapt volume for on-chain volume. Force Index on BTC daily is one of the most reliable crypto indicators.

MACD-HISTOGRAM SIGNALS — Slope more important than level. Rising = bulls gaining. BEST: Divergence—price new high but MACD-H lower peak = bearish divergence. Wait for second peak/trough before acting. CRYPTO: Use on daily chart for BTC direction. MACD divergence on BTC daily has marked every major cycle top since 2013.

IMPULSE SYSTEM — GREEN (13-EMA rising + MACD-H rising) = buy only. RED (both falling) = sell only. BLUE (conflicting) = caution, no new positions. NEVER go long on red bars, never short on green. CRYPTO: Apply to 4H BTC chart for swing entries. Green bars in uptrend = buy dips. Red in downtrend = sell rallies. Blue = wait.

HMM REGIME DETECTION — Hidden Markov Models classify market into regimes (Crash/Bear/Neutral/Bull/Euphoria). HMMs predict TYPE of ENVIRONMENT, not prices. Train on ~2 years daily data. Test 3-7 regimes, pick optimal via BIC. CRITICAL: Use FORWARD ALGORITHM ONLY—default predict() uses entire sequence (look-ahead bias). Regime must persist 3 consecutive bars before acting. If flickers >4 times in 20 bars = UNCERTAINTY, reduce sizes.

VOLATILITY BREAKOUT ENTRY — Enter when price moves > N×ATR from prior close. If close > open + 1.5×ATR, buy. If close < open - 1.5×ATR, sell. Filter: ADX(14) > 25 for trend confirmation. Tested: ADX filter improved win rate 8-12%, reduced max DD 15-20%. CRYPTO: Use 2×ATR threshold (higher baseline vol). Best on BTC daily and 4H. Combine with volume spike >150% of 20-day average.

CHANNEL BREAKOUT — Buy when price exceeds N-bar highest high, sell short when below N-bar lowest low. Best: 20-55 day lookback for daily bars. Close-only variant reduces whipsaws. 20-bar close-only breakout profitable on 70%+ of markets over 15-year test across 36 futures. Add volatility filter: only trade when ATR > N-day average ATR. CRYPTO: Use 20-day on BTC daily. Use 10-day on 4H for altcoins.

DYNAMIC ATR TRAILING STOP — Exit when price retraces N×ATR from highest high since entry. Chandelier: 3×ATR(22) below highest high. Outperforms fixed stops—adapts to volatility. Best N = 2-4 for daily. Below 2× = too tight, above 4× = too loose. CRYPTO: Use 2.5×ATR(14) for BTC (tighter due to 24/7 vol). Trail on 4H closes, not intraday wicks.

BOLLINGER BAND SQUEEZE — BB inside Keltner Channels = squeeze (low volatility, energy building). When BB expands outside Keltner = squeeze FIRES. Only take in direction of dominant trend. CRYPTO: BB squeezes on BTC 4H/daily precede 15-40% moves. Set alerts for squeeze conditions. Wait for breakout direction, then enter with momentum confirmation.

PIVOT POINT TRADING — Pivot = (H+L+C)/3. R1 = 2×Pivot-Low. S1 = 2×Pivot-High. R2/S2 = Pivot ± (H-L). Markets test daily pivot 90% of the time. R2/S2 = dead high/low (90% won't close beyond). NEVER fade R3/S3. Stop after 2 consecutive losers. CRYPTO: Calculate pivots from UTC midnight. BTC tests daily pivot ~85% of the time.

FIBONACCI RETRACEMENT — After trend impulse, correction ends at 38.2% (shallow/strong trend), 50% (moderate), 61.8% (deep/weak trend). Alternate projections (127.2%, 161.8%, 200%, 261.8%) qualify which level holds. Multiple Fib levels converging = high probability reversal target. Apply Fib to TIME as well as price. Price + time confluence = highest probability. CRYPTO: 0.618 is the most common BTC pullback during trends.

DUAL TIMEFRAME MOMENTUM — Use two timeframes (daily + weekly). Both must show momentum reversal in same direction for signal. Daily alone = too many false signals. Weekly alone = too slow. Combined = high probability. Use Rate-of-Change or MACD on both. CRYPTO: Use daily + 12H for BTC swing trades.

MULTIPLE-UNIT EXIT — Divide position into 2-3 units. First target: 1:1 R/R (move stop to breakeven). Second: prior swing high/low or 2:1 R/R. Third: trail with 2-bar reversal or until momentum reverses on higher timeframe. CRYPTO: More aggressive scale-out—take 50% at 1:1, 25% at 2:1, trail 25%.

ADX TREND FILTER — Rising ADX = trend strengthening. Falling = weakening. ADX > 25 = trending, use trend-following tools. ADX < 20 = ranging, use oscillators. CRYPTO: ADX on BTC daily reliably identifies trending vs ranging. When ADX > 30, trend following dominates. When ADX < 20, mean reversion outperforms.

STOCHASTIC — 5-7 day window. Reference lines at 15 (oversold) and 85 (overbought). BUY on bullish divergence near lower line. SELL on bearish divergence near upper. CRITICAL: Do NOT buy above 85 or short below 15. CRYPTO: Use 14-period on BTC daily. Oversold < 20, overbought > 80 in strong trends (wider than traditional).

RSI — Overbought >70, oversold <30. Best for divergences. In strong trends, RSI stays extended. Uptrend range: 40-80 (40 = oversold). Downtrend: 20-60 (60 = overbought). 30/70 only for ranging. CRYPTO: BTC in bull trends rarely reaches RSI 30—40-45 is the pullback buy zone. In bear trends, RSI 60-65 is the sell zone.

VWAP — Price above = bullish, below = bearish. Institutional execution benchmark. Use as dynamic S/R. In trending days, price stays on one side all day. In choppy, oscillates around VWAP. CRYPTO: Calculate from UTC 00:00. BTC respects VWAP as intraday S/R ~70% of the time. Best on 5-min and 15-min charts.

THREE ESSENTIAL PATTERNS — (1) Trending move: 5 waves, no wave 4 overlap of wave 1. (2) Simple correction: ABC, 3 waves. (3) Complex correction: WXY, triangles. If you can identify these 3, you know whether to trade with or against the move. Wave 5 key: When reaches 61.8-100% extension AND momentum divergence = trend ending. CRYPTO: Elliott Wave works well on BTC weekly/daily. Wave 3 extensions common (1.618× or 2.618× Wave 1).

ABCD PATTERN — Impulse A-B, pullback to C (38-50% Fib), continuation to D. Entry at C with MA/VWAP support. Stop below C. Target = AB leg extension (127.2-161.8%). CRYPTO: Very common on BTC 4H. Entry at 38.2% in strong trend, 50% moderate, 61.8% weak. Pullback should have declining volume, continuation expanding volume.

CUP AND HANDLE — U-shaped rounded bottom + short downward handle = bullish reversal. Enter above handle breakout. Target = cup depth projected above breakout. Handle forms on declining volume, breakout on volume surge. BTC cup-and-handle formations take weeks to months on daily. Cup depth typically 20-30%.

WEDGE PATTERNS — RISING WEDGE = BEARISH: Higher highs + higher lows converging. Enter short below wedge. Target = opening width. FALLING WEDGE = BULLISH: Lower highs + lower lows. Enter long above upper trendline. CRYPTO: Rising wedges on BTC daily often precede 20-40% corrections. Falling wedges at bear market bottoms mark cycle lows.

TRIANGLE PATTERNS — ASCENDING: Higher lows + flat resistance. Bullish continuation. DESCENDING: Flat support + lower highs. Bearish. SYMMETRICAL: Direction follows origin trend. Target = triangle height from breakout. Volume decreases during formation, expands on breakout. CRYPTO: Ascending triangles before BTC halving have historically resolved upward explosively.

DOUBLE TOP/BOTTOM — Two approximately equal peaks/troughs with S/R between. Confirmed on break with increased volume. Watch for indicator divergence at second peak. Short target = distance from support to resistance projected downward. CRYPTO: Double tops at round numbers ($50K, $100K BTC) are particularly significant due to psychological resistance and options strike clustering.

KANGAROO TAILS — Three-bar reversal: narrow bar, extremely wide bar (tail), narrow bar. BUY when tail hangs down. SHORT when tail points up. Shows market tested a level and rejected it. CRYPTO: Especially significant on BTC 4H and daily after extended moves. Long lower wick after decline = buyers stepped in. Long upper wick after rally = sellers overwhelmed.

FALSE BREAKOUT TRADING — Professional fishing expeditions to run stops. BEST TRADES occur after false breakouts—trade back into range with stop at extreme. If stopped, re-enter. Professionals attempt multiple entries. CRYPTO: BTC false breakouts above key levels ($50K, $100K) are among the highest-probability fade setups.

SUPPORT/RESISTANCE PSYCHOLOGY — Former support becomes resistance and vice versa. Emotional memory. Draw lines across congestion area EDGES, not extreme points. S/R are flexible like wire fences, not glass walls. Tested levels are STRONGER (more orders cluster). CRYPTO: Prior cycle highs/lows become major S/R. On-chain cost-basis levels create 'real' S/R.

INTERMARKET CHAIN — Bonds lead stocks, stocks lead commodities, commodities lead currencies. Key: S&P-DAX 0.85+, Gold-Dollar -0.6 to -0.8, VIX-Stock -0.75. Correlation instability = regime change. Use rolling 50/200-day correlation. CRYPTO: BTC-NASDAQ correlation fluctuates 0.3-0.8. During risk-off, crypto correlates with risk assets. Monitor BTC/SPX ratio as macro divergence indicator.

SECTOR ROTATION — Early: XLF, XLY. Mid: XLK, XLI. Late: XLE, XLB. Recession: XLU, XLP, XLV. Monthly RS rebalancing leads price 1-3 months. CRYPTO: Early bull = BTC leads. Mid bull = ETH + L1s. Late bull = DeFi + meme coins. Bear = stablecoins + BTC dominance rises. Use BTC dominance index as rotation indicator.

SIMPLE REGIME FILTER — SPY > 200-day SMA + 50 > 200 = BULL (long only). SPY < 200 + 50 < 200 = BEAR (short or cash). Mixed = reduce 50%. CRYPTO: BTC > 200-day SMA = bull, favor longs. BTC < 200 = bear, reduce exposure. BTC dominance rising + BTC below 200 SMA = worst for alts. Add: 50 EMA > 200 EMA AND ADX > 25 = strong bull.

MARKET CYCLE FOUR PHASES — (1) ACCUMULATION: Smart money buying, sideways, low volume. (2) MARKUP: Uptrend begins, rising volume + price. (3) DISTRIBUTION: Smart money selling, high volume + sideways. (4) MARKDOWN: Downtrend, rising volume + falling price. CRYPTO: BTC halving cycle creates predictable phase transitions. Post-halving accumulation → markup (12-18 months) → distribution → markdown. Use on-chain metrics (exchange flows, MVRV) to identify phase.

WYCKOFF ACCUMULATION/DISTRIBUTION — Smart money accumulates during bear markets when volume low, prices depressed. Distributes during bull markets when volume high, prices elevated. Track exchange outflows (accumulation) and inflows (distribution). CRYPTO: On-chain exchange flow data makes Wyckoff more transparent in crypto than any traditional market.

BUBBLE STRUCTURE — (1) Displacement: new technology. (2) Boom: prices rise, media amplifies. (3) Euphoria: 'this time is different,' FOMO retail. (4) Profit-taking: smart money exits. (5) Panic: mass selling. When taxi drivers give crypto tips = late stage 3/early stage 4. Scale out 10% per 20% rise above fair-value estimate. Each cycle faster, more institutional. Post-halving blow-off tops have diminishing returns.

CRYPTO SENTIMENT INDICATORS — Funding Rate: >0.1%/8hr = extreme long crowded (bearish). <-0.05% = extreme short (bullish). MVRV Z-Score: >7 overvalued, <0 undervalued. SOPR: >1.1 overheated, <0.98 capitulation. Stablecoin dominance: rising = risk-off. BTC dominance: rising = alt weakness. Fear & Greed: <20 = buy, >80 = sell. Use 3+ indicators for confirmation.

CONTRARIAN SENTIMENT — When 80%+ bullish = near top. When 80%+ bearish = near bottom. Crowd is right during trends, ALWAYS wrong at extremes. When entire CT timeline is bullish = danger. When everyone terrified = opportunity. CRYPTO: Fear & Greed Index, funding rates, social sentiment. Extreme readings on 3+ indicators = high-probability contrarian entry.

BITCOIN HALVING CYCLE — Block rewards halve every ~4 years. Pattern: 12-18 months pre-halving accumulation, parabolic run 6-12 months post-halving, then 80%+ bear market correction. Each cycle's return diminishes. Halving reduces new supply—supply shock drives narrative and price. CRYPTO: Plan for 3-4 year round trips. Accumulate during bear (12-18 months before halving). Reduce exposure 6-12 months after as euphoria builds.

BTC CORRELATION CASCADE — Most cryptos tied to BTC. If BTC plummets, all alts follow with amplified downside. Altcoins = leveraged bet on BTC direction + idiosyncratic risk. Monitor BTC dominance: rising = alts underperform; falling = alt season begins. CRYPTO: When BTC dominance rising + BTC trending up = only hold BTC. When dominance falling + BTC stable = rotate into quality alts. When BTC falling = everything falls, go to stables.

LONG STRADDLE/STRANGLE — Buy straddle (ATM call + put) before major events (halvings, ETF decisions, FOMC). Strangles (OTM) cheaper but need larger moves. Key: Buy when IV rank <30%, sell when IV rank >70%. CRYPTO: On Deribit, buy BTC straddle 7-14 days before major events. Close 1 day after. Average IV expansion 20-40%. Max loss = premium paid.

COVERED CALL ON CRYPTO — Sell OTM calls against long BTC/ETH to generate yield. Collects premium from high crypto IV. Risk: capped upside. Best during ranging/consolidation when IV elevated. On Deribit, weekly/monthly covered calls yield 1-5% monthly. Sell 30-delta calls, 30-45 DTE. Close at 50% profit or roll when tested. Max 10% of portfolio.

PROTECTIVE PUT — Buy OTM puts as crash insurance. BTC can drop 30% overnight. Best risk/reward: buy when IV rank <20% (complacency). Avoid when IV extreme (overpaying). Collar = sell call to finance put. CRYPTO: Buy deep OTM BTC puts (5-10% OTM) when IV rank <20%. Accept most expire worthless—few that pay cover all costs. Size: 1-3% of portfolio per month on tail hedges.

IRON CONDOR — Sell OTM put spread + sell OTM call spread. Net credit. Max profit = credit. Max loss = width-credit. Close at 50% profit or 2× credit stop. Best when IV rank >50. Use 16-delta strikes (~68% PoP). CRYPTO: On Deribit BTC options. 30-45 DTE. Close/roll at 21 DTE to avoid gamma risk. IV floor ~50% means more premium but more risk. Size for 3σ moves.

IMPLIED VOLATILITY REGIME — IV rank <20% = complacency, buy vol (straddles). IV rank >80% = panic, sell vol (iron condors, covered calls). Crypto IV has floor ~50% annualized and spikes to 150%+ during crashes. Mean-reversion in IV is one of the most reliable patterns in crypto options. When IV > RV by 10+ points, systematically sell premium. When IV < RV, buy premium. Size for catastrophic tail risk.

OPTION GREEKS FOR CRYPTO — Delta = directional exposure. Gamma = delta change rate (highest ATM near expiry). Theta = time decay (accelerates near expiry). Vega = vol sensitivity (highest longer-dated). Crypto vega risk enormous due to IV spikes. Gamma explodes final 48 hours. Crypto options expire 08:00 UTC Friday on Deribit. Close/roll at 21 DTE. Never hold short options through expiry week.

OPTION SYNTHETICS — Long call + short put at same strike = synthetic long spot (cheaper than buying BTC). Avoids funding rate bleed. In crypto perps, funding can be 0.1%+/8hr during euphoria. Use when funding expensive and IV skew moderate. CRYPTO: When funding >0.05%/8hr, switch from perps to synthetic options positions.

HMM FEATURE ENGINEERING — Input: price action (returns, volatility, trend strength), volume (relative volume, OBV), volatility (ATR, realized vol, GARCH). Quality of regime detection depends on feature selection. Train on 2 years daily. Test 3-7 regimes, pick via BIC. CRITICAL: Forward algorithm only. Regime must persist 3 bars before acting.

VOLATILITY-BASED ALLOCATION — Low vol → 95% allocation, 1.25× leverage. Medium → variable, 1.0× leverage, stay if trend intact. High → minimal, no leverage, capital preservation. Allocation influenced by regime detection. CRYPTO: 30-day realized vol. <40% annualized = low (max allocation). 40-80% = medium (reduced). >80% = high (minimal). >120% = crisis (cash/stables).

RANDOM ENTRY TEST — Use random coin-flip entries to isolate exit and sizing performance. Random entry + trend filter (350/25 EMA) generated 32.46% CAGR with 43.74% max DD over 10 years. Best random: 53.3%, worst: 17.5%. Proves trend filter alone has massive edge—entry timing is secondary to trading with the trend. CRYPTO: Run random entry tests on BTC data to validate exits and sizing.

WALK-FORWARD OPTIMIZATION — Rolling-window backtesting. Process: (1) Optimize on in-sample (252 days). (2) Validate on out-of-sample (~6 months). (3) Record OOS performance. (4) Roll forward. (5) Repeat. Stitch ONLY OOS = realistic live simulation. Target: live Sharpe > 50% of backtest Sharpe. OOS Sharpe >1.0 = viable. CRYPTO: Use 2-year in-sample, 6-month OOS (captures one full cycle).

MONTE CARLO SIMULATION — Randomize trade order 10,000×. Calculate: max DD, final equity, Sharpe for each. 5th percentile DD = worst-case to expect. 50th = median. 95th = best case. If 5th percentile DD >30% = unacceptable tail risk. CRYPTO: Use fat-tailed distributions (Student-t) not normal. If >5% of paths result in >50% DD, sizing too aggressive.

CURVE-FITTING DETECTION — (1) In-sample Sharpe >3.0 with OOS <1.0. (2) >10 parameters. (3) Optimal params change across periods. (4) Works on only ONE asset. (5) Performance degrades with each year. T/P ratio: If trades/params < 20, heavily curve-fit. Target T/P > 50. 5 params needs 250+ trades minimum.

STRATEGY DEGRADATION TRIGGERS — Pause when: (1) Live DD exceeds 2× historical max. (2) Win rate below break-even for 30+ trades. (3) Sharpe <0.5 for 3 months. (4) Concept drift detected. (5) Exchange changed fee/API. Response: Min size → review trades → re-run WFO → resume at reduced size or retire strategy.

PAPER-TO-LIVE TRANSITION — All checked: (1) 30+ days paper trading positive. (2) WFO backtest OOS Sharpe >1.0. (3) Stress test passed (max DD <25%). (4) Execution latency <500ms. (5) Error handling tested. (6) Circuit breakers tested. (7) 50+ paper trades. Scale: Start 25% intended size → 50% after 20 profitable live → 100% after 50 profitable live. CRYPTO: Paper trade through at least one major volatility event.

BLACK SWAN STRESS TESTING — (1) Inject random 10-15% crashes. (2) Test COVID crash, Terra/Luna, FTX collapse. (3) Monte Carlo 10,000× for 5th percentile DD. (4) Test with 2× historical volatility. Robust strategy survives ALL with DD <25%. If any produces >40% DD = NOT production-ready. CRYPTO: Add stablecoin depeg and exchange hack scenarios.

EXPECTANCY & R-MULTIPLES — Expectancy = (Win% × Avg Win) - (Loss% × Avg Loss). Must be positive. R-multiples: R = initial risk. Track distribution of all R outcomes. SQN = (Avg R / Std Dev R) × √(trades). SQN >1.6 = tradeable. >2.0 = good. >3.0 = excellent. 40% WR with 3:1 R/R = 0.60 expectancy/dollar.

POSITION SIZING MODELS — (1) Percent Risk: Equity × Risk% ÷ (Entry - Stop) = position. 0.5-1% most popular. (2) Percent Volatility: Equity × Vol% ÷ ATR = position. Equalizes risk. (3) Kelly: f* = W - (1-W)/R. Use half-Kelly. Position sizing accounts for MOST variation in performance. Same system, 5 models: No sizing = 1.75% annual. 1% risk = 20.92%. Position sizing transformed identical system from mediocre to spectacular.

DRAWDOWN vs RECOVERY — 10% loss needs 11% gain. 20% needs 25%. 30% needs 43%. 40% needs 67%. 50% needs 100%. Beyond 50% = almost impossible recovery. Know your max DD tolerance BEFORE trading. Most overestimate. Psychological damage is non-linear—20% feels more than twice as bad as 10%. CRYPTO: A 50% loss needs 100% gain. Use stops.

ANTI-MARTINGALE SIZING — Martingale (increase after loss) = dangerous → ruin. Anti-martingale (increase after win) = correct. ALL professional sizing is anti-martingale. Vince study: 40 PhDs played positive-expectancy game; 95% LOST due to poor sizing. Never double down after losses. Increase only after new equity highs. Reduce during drawdowns.

CORRELATION RISK — Don't hold 10 crypto positions all 80%+ correlated to BTC. That's concentrated directional risk with 10× costs. If >0.7, treat as one position. In crisis, ALL correlations → 1.0. CRYPTO: BTC/ETH/SOL/AVAX = one risk factor. DeFi = one risk factor. True diversification = non-crypto assets. Max 3-5 positions in same correlation cluster. Monitor 30-day rolling correlation.

SURVIVAL RULES — (1) Strategy with 99% win rate but 1% total ruin is a time bomb. (2) Ergodicity: ensemble average ≠ time average. If one bad sequence leads to ruin, you're eliminated. (3) Never cross a river averaging 4 feet deep—averages hide extremes. (4) Tail risk IS the business in crypto. Size for the worst day, not the average day.

CRYPTO LEVERAGE LIMITS — LTCM used 25:1 and blew up. Crypto offers 100×—far more dangerous. Maximum: 3-5× core positions, 10× highest-conviction short-term only. With 50% WR, 1:1 R/R, 2% risk: 13% chance of 50% DD. With 10% risk: 99% chance. Even 'safe' 2% becomes 20% effective risk with 10× leverage. Keep effective risk <2% of total capital.

CRYPTO EXCHANGE RISK — FTX collapse showed counterparty risk is existential. (1) Never >20% on single exchange. (2) Cold storage for non-trading capital. (3) Monitor proof-of-reserves. (4) Diversify across 3+ exchanges. (5) Withdraw profits weekly. (6) Never use exchange tokens as collateral. (7) Emergency withdrawal plan. Split: 80% cold storage, 20% active trading across multiple exchanges.

FUNDING RATE AS REGIME SIGNAL — Positive funding = longs pay shorts → reflexivity loop. When >0.1%/8hr sustained, loop at maximum. Unwinding is violent—triggers cascading liquidations. Negative = potential short squeeze. CRYPTO: Monitor across exchanges. Extreme positive = reduce long exposure. Extreme negative = potential long entry. Divergence between exchanges = arbitportunity.

LIQUIDATION CASCADES — When BTC drops, liquidation engines sell into falling prices, creating death spiral. DeFi auto-liquidates collateral, adding fuel. One protocol's failure cascades through ecosystem (Terra → 3AC → Celsius → Voyager). Reduce leverage BEFORE volatility spikes. Monitor liquidation heatmaps. Track aggregate OI and funding. When OI at extremes, system is fragile—happens WEEKLY in crypto.

CRYPTO WALLET SECURITY — Hardware wallets (Ledger/Trezor) = most secure for long-term holds. Paper = secure if offline. Desktop = good security. Mobile = convenient for small amounts. Exchange = highest risk, only for active trading. Verify ALL URLs character-by-character—phishing is #1 cause of crypto loss. Never use Google to navigate to exchanges—type URLs directly or use bookmarks.

CRYPTO ORDER TYPES — Market: instant fill, uncertain price (urgent exits only). Limit: exact price (planned entries). Stop-limit: triggers limit when stop hits (automated risk management). Always use stop-losses. In high-vol crypto, slippage can be 1-3% on low-cap alts. Use OCO orders for simultaneous stop-loss + take-profit. Use iceberg orders for large positions to hide true size.

BTC-DENOMINATED THINKING — When trading alts, profit in satoshis matters more than USD. If alt rises in USD but falls vs BTC, it was a bad trade. Always measure alt performance in satoshi terms. If alt returns 20% USD but BTC returned 30%, you lost 10% relative. Use BTC as benchmark. Only hold alts when outperforming BTC on risk-adjusted basis.

CRYPTO DCA — Allocate fixed USD weekly/monthly regardless of price. Smooths entry volatility. Use exchange recurring buys. DCA removes timing risk but not directional risk. CRYPTO: Best during bear markets. Combine with value averaging: buy more when MVRV <1 (undervalued), less when MVRV >3 (overvalued).

NEURAL NETWORK ENTRIES/EXITS — Feed-forward NN on technical indicators. Use small networks (3-5 nodes) to avoid overfitting. OOS testing mandatory. NN exits outperformed fixed rules by 15-25% in risk-adjusted returns. Retrain quarterly. If spectacular backtest results, probably overfit. Be very skeptical.

GENETIC ALGORITHM — Evolve rule-based templates. Rules like 'If indicator_A > threshold AND indicator_B < threshold THEN buy.' GA searches millions of combinations. Outperforms brute-force optimization. Large OOS window required. Evolved rules must make economic sense. GA frequently finds non-obvious relationships humans miss.

STABLECOIN DYNAMICS — USDT, USDC, DAI maintain 1:1 USD peg. Stablecoins = crypto's cash equivalent. Monitor stablecoin dominance: rising = risk-off; falling = risk-on. Depeg events create systemic cascading liquidations. CRYPTO: Rising dominance during BTC drawdown = potential buying power on sidelines. Falling during rally = capital deploying into risk.

REFLEXIVITY THEORY — Perceptions SHAPE reality. Rising prices → more users → better fundamentals → higher prices (virtuous). Falling → exodus → worse fundamentals → lower (vicious). Crypto is MORE reflexive than any traditional market. When price diverges from fundamentals (dev activity, TVL, addresses), the gap will close—usually price adjusts down. Trade WITH bias until extreme, then fade.

BOOM-BUST PHASES — (1) Trend not yet recognized. (2) Tested and survives. (3) Increasing conviction. (4) Growing perception-reality divergence. (5) Recognition of divergence. (6) Trend reverses. Phase 1-3 = trend follow. Phase 4 = reduce size. Phase 5-6 = reverse or exit. Phase 4 indicators: narrative exhaustion, leverage extremes, retail all-in.

LOSS AVERSION — Humans feel losses 2.5× more than gains. Causes: holding losers too long, cutting winners too short. Counter: automate exits with predetermined stops and targets. Pre-define entries, stops, targets, size BEFORE trade. When emotional, you are the signal to STOP trading. Walk away. Come back with plan, not feelings.

COGNITIVE BIASES — (1) Anchoring: 'market doesn't know what you paid.' (2) Loss aversion: hold losers. (3) Recency bias: overweight recent trades. (4) Confirmation bias. (5) Overconfidence: increase size after wins. (6) Hindsight bias. (7) Narrative fallacy: stories for randomness. Systematic trading eliminates ALL by replacing judgment with rules.

PROCESS VS OUTCOME — A good trade can lose money. A bad trade can make money. Judge by whether you FOLLOWED YOUR SYSTEM, not P&L. Dennis: 'The goal is to make money, but the process is to follow rules.' If you follow rules and lose, it's a good trade. Break rules and win, it's a bad trade—it reinforces rule-breaking. Individual trades meaningless—need 100+ for edge to manifest.

PATIENCE AND DISCIPLINE — 90% of traders lose. The 10% who win have internalized patience and discipline. Imperfect signals + perfect discipline beats brilliant signals + no discipline. Boring is profitable. Excitement = gambling. In crypto's 24/7 market, most is noise. Wait for A+ setups. Sitting in cash IS a position. The hardest trade is often no trade.

CROWD PSYCHOLOGY — When you join a crowd, your IQ drops. Crypto Twitter/Discord are crowd amplification machines. Euphoric crowd wants you to buy at top. Terrified crowd wants you to sell at bottom. Separate yourself. Think independently. Use crowd as CONTRARY indicator. Crowd is right during trends, ALWAYS wrong at extremes.

FOMO CONTROL — FOMO has caused more crypto losses than any indicator failure. FOMO at peaks, into unvetted alts, FOMO leverage. Antidote: there is ALWAYS another trade. Missing one pump doesn't end your career. Blowing up from FOMO DOES. If you missed entry, let it go. Chasing at extended prices violates R/R.

THREE Ms — Mind (disciplined psychology), Method (technical analysis), Money (management). Without all three, success impossible. Amateurs think about profits; professionals about losses. When you feel fear or joy, tighten discipline. Your system was built when calm—follow it when emotional. Self-knowledge comes BEFORE system design.

TRADING JOURNAL — Track: date, instrument, direction, entry/exit price, stop, target, % P&L, R-multiple, commission, net. Include: screens triggered, emotional state, market conditions. Score on TWO scales: profitability AND execution quality. Track impulse plays separately. Review weekly. Record reasons BEFORE entry, not after. Journal is your edge—it reveals behavioral patterns.

PRE-TRADING ACTION PLAN — Write before each session: (1) Market conditions. (2) Key levels. (3) Indicator readings. (4) Potential setups with specific triggers. (5) Position sizes pre-calculated. (6) Stops/targets defined. (7) Max trades for the day. (8) Conditions that keep you OUT. CRYPTO: Since 24/7, set specific trading hours. Don't trade full 24 hours.

TRADE MANAGEMENT HIERARCHY — (1) Never violate risk per trade. (2) Never violate total monthly risk. (3) Only trade with weekly trend. (4) Wait for daily oscillator pullback. (5) Confirm with Impulse System. (6) Enter with limit order. (7) Set stop BEFORE entry. (8) Take partial profits at first target. (9) Trail remainder. (10) Exit if weekly trend reverses.

CRYPTO 24/7 ADAPTATIONS — No open/close = no natural stopping point. Set specific hours to prevent burnout. No PDT rule = unlimited day trades (advantage). No uptick rule = easier to short. Funding rates affect carry. Weekend liquidity thinner = larger spreads, more manipulation. CME futures close weekends = gaps. Define your hours. Treat it like a job. The market will be there tomorrow. Your capital might not be if you trade 24/7.

ON-CHAIN METRICS — MVRV Z-Score: >7 overvalued, <0 undervalued. SOPR: >1.1 overheated, <0.98 capitulation. NVT: High = overvalued vs transaction volume. Exchange inflows: Large BTC to exchanges = selling pressure. Outflows = accumulation. Active addresses growth flattening despite price rise = distribution. Realized price = market's aggregate cost basis—major S/R.

BTC OPTIONS EXPIRY EFFECTS — Large expiries (monthly, quarterly) create pin risk and gamma effects around max-pain. MMs delta-hedge short options, creating flow. Week before large expiry (>$1B notional), price gravitates toward max-pain. Post-expiry, flow reverses—often causing directional move. Expire 08:00 UTC Friday on Deribit. Gamma explodes final 48 hours. Close/roll at 21 DTE.

ALTCOIN CLASSIFICATION — L1s (ETH, SOL, AVAX) compete on smart contract platforms. L2s (MATIC, ARB) scale L1s. DeFi (UNI, AAVE) power financial protocols. Memecoins (DOGE, SHIB) trade on narrative. Infrastructure (LINK, DOT) provides middleware. Each has different risk/reward and BTC correlation. Diversify across categories. CRYPTO: During alt season, L1s lead first, then DeFi, then memes last. Memes topping = cycle late stage.

NEWS SENTIMENT MOMENTUM — NLP sentiment scores predict short-term momentum. Positive = buy, negative = sell. Combine with traditional momentum for stronger signal. Decay is fast—hold days, not weeks. CRYPTO: Crypto Twitter sentiment, Reddit activity, Google Trends. Combine 3+ sources. Sentiment + price momentum > either alone.

CRYPTO SCAM AWARENESS — If too good to be true, it is. Verify ALL URLs character-by-character. Never use Google to navigate to exchanges. Phishing = #1 cause of crypto loss. Pump-and-dump signs: unexplained volume in low-cap, social media hype, 500%+ rise in hours, insiders sell into retail, 90%+ collapse.

CRYPTO DIP PSYCHOLOGY — BTC periodically dips then recovers to new ATHs. Pattern trains HODLers to buy dips—each cycle conditions more complacency. Eventually a dip doesn't recover for years (2018, 2022). Track on-chain metrics (MVRV, SOPR) to distinguish corrections from bear markets. Don't blindly BTFD without data. 30% dip in bull market = opportunity. In bear market = beginning.

EXIT EVALUATION — Use random entries to isolate exit performance. If exit profits from random entries, it has edge. SES: Fixed stop (3×ATR) + target (6×ATR) + time limit (10 bars). Exits MORE important than entries for profitability. Test exits independently. CRYPTO: Test on BTC with random entries. If exit can't profit from random BTC entries, it has no edge.

CRYPTO BUBBLE INDICATORS — (1) Rapid price increase, no fundamental change. (2) Media explosion. (3) 'New era' thinking. (4) Inexperienced participants increasing. (5) Derivatives speculation surge. All five present = mania. Count them. More = closer to top. CRYPTO: All five + taxi driver tips = late stage. Scale out 10% per 20% rise above fair-value.

FEAR CONTAGION — Fear spreads faster than greed. One exchange collapse creates panic across ALL crypto—guilt by association. Use fear events to buy fundamentally sound assets at irrational discounts, but ONLY AFTER initial panic subsides (48-72 hours minimum). Best buying when on-chain shows maximum pain (SOPR <0.85, MVRV <0.5, exchange outflows surging).

SURVIVORSHIP BIAS — You only see winners. For every BTC millionaire, thousands lost on dead alts. 95%+ of altcoins go to zero. When backtesting, include dead coins. Testing only on survivors inflates results 30-50%+. When evaluating strategies, ask: what happened to those who tried and failed? Their silence is informative.

NOISE VS SIGNAL — Daily price movements are mostly noise. Watching 1-min charts creates emotional reactions to randomness. S/N ratio improves with longer timeframes. Check prices less frequently. Set alerts at key levels. Media explanations of daily moves are post-hoc narrative fitting. CRYPTO: Check BTC at specific times (UTC 00:00, 12:00), not continuously.

MULTIPLE EXIT STRATEGY — (1) Catastrophic stop (wide, vol-based). (2) Trailing stop (locks profit, lets winners run). (3) Time stop (exit if no profit in X periods). (4) Profit target (partial position). Each serves different purpose. Simplicity in each = robustness. CRYPTO: Use OCO orders for simultaneous stop-loss + take-profit. Automates exits, removes emotion.

CORRELATION CONVERGENCE IN CRISIS — In 2008, 'uncorrelated' assets all collapsed together. In crypto, same—BTC, ETH, DeFi, even 'uncorrelated' algo stables crash simultaneously. Diversification within crypto provides less protection than assumed. True diversification = non-crypto assets. CRYPTO: Assume ALL positions perfectly correlated during crisis. Size for scenario where ALL drop 50% simultaneously.

FED POLICY AND CRYPTO — Loose monetary policy (low rates, QE) feeds speculative bubbles. Tightening punctures them. 2020-2021 crypto bubble = zero rates + massive QE. 2022 tightening collapsed crypto 75%+. CRYPTO: BTC has led NASDAQ by 2-3 months in recent cycles. When BTC diverges from risk-on assets, pay attention—either signaling macro shift or about to re-correlate.

VOLATILITY SELLING IN CRYPTO — IV structurally elevated (50-100% annualized for BTC). Creates persistent premium-selling opportunities. Strategies: cash-secured puts, iron condors, covered calls. When IV > RV by 10+ points, systematically sell premium. Risk: black swan catastrophic losses. Size for 3σ moves. Never sell naked options in crypto. Max loss = defined spread width.

SCALPER RULES — Use tick chart (144-tick or 233-tick). Enter on buy/sell signal at pivot levels. First target: 1-2 points on half. Move stop to breakeven after first target. Trail on remainder. After 2 consecutive losers, done for the day. Scalps generate monthly income; swings create wealth. CRYPTO: Use 1-min on BTC perpetual. Target 0.1-0.3% per scalp. Max 10 scalps/day.

CRYPTO PROTOCOL RISK — 51% attack: >50% hash power can manipulate transactions. Smaller PoW chains vulnerable. Any credible 51% report = immediate sell. Smart contract risk: audits don't guarantee safety. DeFi exploits drain liquidity overnight. Never >5% in any single DeFi protocol. Track TVL, audit status, insurance coverage before deploying capital.

CRYPTO ADOPTION METRICS — Network effect: value grows as adoption increases. Track: new wallet addresses, active addresses, transaction count, hash rate, developer activity (GitHub), TVL. Price rising but adoption flattening = distribution. Price falling but adoption holding = accumulation. Provides pseudo-fundamental valuation unique to crypto.

BUY/SELL WALLS — Buy wall = large buy orders creating support. Sell wall = large sell orders creating resistance. Walls can be spoofed. Track persistence and fill rates. Real walls persist >30 minutes and absorb market orders. Fake walls disappear when approached. CRYPTO: Use order book depth analysis on multiple exchanges.

CRYPTO CARRY TRADES — Funding rate arbitrage: Long spot + short perp when funding positive (collect funding). Basis trade: Exploit spot-futures spread. Annualized yield can be 10-50%+ during bull markets. Risk: exchange counterparty, funding reversal, liquidation cascade. When funding >0.1%/8hr sustained, carry yield is attractive. Size for funding reversal scenarios.

CRYPTO INDEX BENCHMARKING — CCI30 tracks top 30 by market cap. If portfolio underperforms index, better off holding index. In bull markets, alt selection adds value. In bear markets, index with reduced size minimizes drawdowns. Most altcoin pickers underperform holding BTC/ETH. Equivalent: 60% BTC, 30% ETH, 10% top-10 alts. Rebalance quarterly.

CRYPTO EXECUTION OPTIMIZATION — MEV on DEXs: bots front-run large swaps. Use limit orders, not market. Slippage: 0.5-1% for majors, 2-5% for low-cap. On CEXs: iceberg orders for large positions. TWAP/VWAP for institutional size. Split across exchanges. Avoid low-liquidity periods. Use aggregators (1inch, Paraswap) for best DEX execution.

CRYPTO REGULATORY RISK — SEC actions, exchange bans, regulatory tightening puncture bubbles. Soros's credit cycle applies: easy money → leverage → asset inflation → regulators notice → tightening → forced deleveraging → crash. Reduce exposure when regulatory rhetoric intensifies. CRYPTO: Regulatory response to mania is predictable—prepare for it.

CME BTC FUTURES GAPS — CME closes weekends, creating gaps on reopen. Gaps fill ~70% of the time. Monday gaps reveal institutional sentiment. Large gaps in trend direction = continuation. CRYPTO: Monitor CME BTC and ETH gaps. Small gaps (<0.5%) fill almost always. Large gaps (>2%) may not fill for weeks. Trade fills during Sunday evening/Monday morning.

PROTOCOL REVENUE ANALYSIS — Owner earnings = NI + D&A - CapEx. Crypto equivalent: protocol revenue - emissions = sustainable yield. Track: fee revenue, token emissions, treasury runway. Protocols with real revenue and declining emissions = sustainable. Protocols relying on emissions to attract users = Ponzi-like. Use to filter altcoin investments.

LEVERAGE AS SYSTEMIC RISK — Excessive leverage was the detonator in 2008 and every crypto crash. When aggregate leverage (OI, funding rates, margin debt) reaches extremes, system is fragile. One catalyst triggers cascading liquidations. Monitor: BTC futures OI, aggregate funding, DeFi utilization. Extreme readings = reduce exposure immediately. CRYPTO: This happens multiple times per year, not just in major crises.

COMPOUND GROWTH MATH — $1M at 20% annual for 20 years = $38.3M. At 25% = $86.7M. At 30% = $190M. Small differences compound enormously. This is why 'boring' 15-20% built $770M+. Goal isn't maximum return—it's maximum sustainable compound growth with tolerable drawdowns. Protect equity above all—it's your working capital.

MECHANICAL VS DISCRETIONARY — Rules for sizing/entry/exit. Discretionary only to REDUCE risk. System traders achieve more consistent results. Professional monitors system like a hawk, knows normal drawdown vs system deterioration. Automatic systems don't work because market follows imperfect laws of mass psychology. Use backtested components but retain judgment. All mechanical systems eventually self-destruct—monitor and adapt.

CRYPTO MARKET MATURITY — Each cycle is structurally different—institutional participation, regulation, infrastructure evolve. Don't assume previous cycle patterns repeat exactly. Adapt framework to current cycle's structure. 2013: retail only. 2017: ICOs + retail. 2021: institutions + DeFi + NFTs. 2025+: ETFs + nation-states + RWA. Edge shifts as market matures. What worked last cycle may not work this one.

TRANSACTION COST REALITY — Seldom see a system producing profits much bigger than its transaction costs. If system generates $1M net, it probably generates >$1M in costs. Factor: commissions, slippage, bid-ask spread, market impact. Calculate costs as % of total P&L. Target: costs <50% of gross profit. CRYPTO: Crypto costs include: exchange fees (0.04-0.1%), slippage (0.05-0.5%), funding rates (variable), withdrawal fees. High-frequency crypto strategies need extremely tight execution.

SIMPLICITY PRINCIPLE — Simple concepts work best. Complexity leads to curve fitting. If you can't explain system in one paragraph, it's too complex. Simple setups, entries, exits, sizing rules. Each component understandable independently. Multiple simple components combined > one complex system. If it breaks under pressure, simplify further. Occam's Razor: simplest system that works is best.

BACKTEST DATA QUALITY — Survivorship bias: exclude delisted stocks/coins → results inflated 5-15% annually. Use survivorship-bias-free data. Futures continuous contract: back-adjusting makes P&L correct but returns incorrect (or vice versa). Choose based on strategy type. Primary vs consolidated quotes: consolidated may differ from primary exchange. Use primary for accuracy. CRYPTO: Include dead altcoins. Use primary exchange data.

OPTIMIZATION PARADOX — Choosing specific parameters (25-day vs 30-day) reduces predictive value. Solution: Use broad parameter ranges that work across many values. If system works for 20-40 day breakout, more robust than one tuned to exactly 23 days. Robust optimum is in middle of flat performance region. If 'best' parameter at extreme of tested range, likely overfit. Fewer parameters = more robust. Target ≤5 parameters.

REGIME SHIFT AWARENESS — Backtest predictive power fails when market structure changes. Examples: 2001 decimalization killed stat arb. 2008 halved volumes hurting mean reversion. 2010 flash crash changed HFT. Always ask: 'Has market structure changed since backtest period?' CRYPTO: Each halving cycle fundamentally changes market structure. Exchange hacks, regulatory changes, new products all alter dynamics. Re-validate strategies after structural breaks.

CONCEPT DRIFT AND MODEL DRIFT — Concept drift = underlying market relationships change (BTC-stock correlation shifts). Model drift = predictive accuracy degrades. Detection: Monitor rolling OOS Sharpe. If drops below 0.5 for 3+ consecutive OOS windows = drift detected. Response: retrain on recent data, reduce size, if persists >6 months strategy may be permanently degraded. Prevention: shorter in-sample windows (2-3 years) to adapt faster.

CORRELATION INSTABILITY — Correlations are NOT constant. Use rolling 50/200-day correlation. Strong correlation break = regime change signal. Gold-Dollar: -0.6 to -0.8. AUD/Gold: 0.7+. CAD/Oil: 0.6+. S&P-DAX: 0.85+. VIX-Stock: -0.75. These ranges shift. CRYPTO: BTC-NASDAQ correlation fluctuates 0.3-0.8 depending on regime. During risk-off, everything correlates. Monitor for divergence as regime change signal.

NARRATIVE ECONOMICS IN CRYPTO — Prices driven by stories, not fundamentals. BTC narratives shift: digital gold → inflation hedge → institutional adoption → national reserve. Each attracts different capital. When narrative exhausts its marginal buyer, price stagnates. Track narrative evolution—if media recycling old narratives, cycle is mature. When narrative shifts from analysis to ideology ('BTC will replace all money') = late stage.

CRYPTO VALUATION MODELS — Stock-to-Flow (scarcity model), NVT Ratio (network value to transactions), Metcalfe's Law (value proportional to users²), Realized Price (aggregate cost basis). When price deviates >2σ from these models, mean-reversion likely within 6-18 months. CRYPTO: These provide pseudo-fundamental anchoring unique to crypto. No earnings or dividends, but network metrics serve similar purpose.

EXCHANGE SOLVENCY MONITORING — After FTX, proof-of-reserves is essential. Check: (1) Regular attestation reports. (2) On-chain wallet verification. (3) Insurance fund size. (4) Regulatory compliance status. (5) Withdrawal processing times (delays = red flag). (6) Social media complaints about withdrawals. If any red flags, reduce exposure immediately. Never keep >20% on any single exchange regardless of reputation.

DeFi RISK MANAGEMENT — Smart contract risk: even audited protocols get exploited. Impermanent loss: providing liquidity can lose vs holding. Oracle risk: price feed manipulation causes cascading liquidations. Rug pulls: anonymous teams can drain liquidity. Rules: (1) Never >5% in single protocol. (2) Prefer audited, battle-tested protocols. (3) Monitor TVL changes. (4) Understand the yield source—unsustainable yield = red flag. (5) Have exit plan before entering.

STABLECOIN RISK — Not all stables are equal. USDT: centralized, opaque reserves. USDC: regulated, transparent but can be frozen. DAI: decentralized, over-collateralized. Algorithmic stables (UST, FRAX): depeg risk during stress. Rules: (1) Diversify across stablecoin types. (2) Never >50% in single stablecoin. (3) Monitor peg deviation—>2% = exit. (4) During depeg events, all crypto correlation → 1.0 (everything falls).

STRATEGY PERFORMANCE MEAN-REVERTS — Strategy with great recent returns will likely underperform going forward. Don't increase allocation to recent winners or abandon recent losers. Equal-weight across strategies is robust. If strategy Sharpe has been 2.0 for 3 years, expect it to drop to 1.0-1.5. Rebalance strategy allocations quarterly, not based on recent performance.

FUNDING RATE ARBITRAGE — When funding >0.1%/8hr: Long spot + short perp = collect funding with no directional risk. Annualized yield can be 20-50%+ during bull markets. Risk: exchange counterparty, funding reversal, spot-perp basis widening. CRYPTO: Monitor funding across Binance, Bybit, OKX. When rates diverge between exchanges, cross-exchange arb opportunity. Size for worst-case scenario where funding reverses before you can unwind.

TIME RETRACEMENTS — Apply Fibonacci ratios to TIME as well as price. Measure duration of prior trend, project correction time at 38.2%, 50%, 61.8%. When price reaches Fib price target AND time target simultaneously = highest probability reversal zone. Time-price confluence is the most powerful setup. CRYPTO: BTC cycles have distinct time characteristics. Bear markets typically last 12-14 months. Time targets from prior cycle peaks project future reversal zones.

TREND FOLLOWING FOUR COMMANDMENTS — (1) Trade with the trend. (2) Cut your losses. (3) Let your profits run. (4) Don't let big profits get away. These rules are 'light years ahead of so many traders.' In crypto, trend following works exceptionally well because crypto trends persist longer than traditional markets due to retail-driven momentum and 24/7 global participation. Accept you won't pick tops or bottoms—capture 60-80% of major trends.

SYSTEM DEVELOPMENT TRUTH — Don't look for 'the' system. Look for ANY system with positive expectancy that you can follow. System contributes ~20% to success. Position sizing ~30%. Psychology ~50%. A mediocre system traded with discipline beats a great system traded emotionally. Focus on process, not outcome. The system is simple. Following it is hard. This is the central lesson of trading.

BTC CYCLE INDICATORS — Pre-halving accumulation: 12-18 months of sideways/declining prices with rising on-chain accumulation. Post-halving markup: 6-12 months of accelerating prices. Blow-off top: parabolic advance, extreme leverage, retail FOMO. Bear market: 80%+ correction, 12-14 months. Key indicators: MVRV Z-Score for valuation, SOPR for profit/loss extremes, exchange flows for smart money behavior, funding rates for leverage.

CRYPTO PORTFOLIO ALLOCATION — Core (60-80%): BTC + ETH. Satellite (20-40%): Quality L1s, DeFi blue chips. Avoid: >10% in any single alt, >5% in unproven protocols. Rebalance quarterly. During bear: increase BTC allocation (70%+). During bull: can increase alt allocation. Always maintain 10-20% stablecoins for dry powder. Cash = call option on crises. In crypto, stablecoins serve this function.

DAILY CIRCUIT BREAKER (CRYPTO) — Daily loss >3% → STOP TRADING for 24 hours. Daily loss >5% → close all positions, take 48-hour break. Three consecutive losing days → mandatory 1-week break. Weekly loss >10% → close everything, paper trade until 20+ profitable simulated trades. Return at half previous size. CRYPTO: These limits must be hardcoded—don't rely on willpower during drawdowns. Use exchange-level position limits where available.

TREND PULLBACK ENTRY — Confirmed uptrend (higher highs/lows, 20 EMA rising, ADX >25). Wait for pullback to support: 10/20 EMA, 38.2-61.8% Fib retracement, or prior swing low. Enter on reversal candle at support. Stop below support. Target 2:1 R/R minimum. CRYPTO: BTC pullbacks to 21-EMA on daily in strong uptrends are among the highest-probability entries. Combine with Force Index dip for timing.

MA CONFLUENCE COMPRESSION — When 10/20/50 EMA converge tightly, a powerful move is coming. Compression after consolidation = coiled spring. Direction determined by larger trend (200 EMA). CRYPTO: BTC MA compressions on daily chart precede 15-30% moves. Set alerts when 10/20/50 EMA spread <3% of price. Enter when price breaks above/below the compressed MAs with volume confirmation.

VOLATILITY REGIME FILTER — VIX <15 = low vol (favor mean reversion). VIX >25 = high vol (favor momentum, reduce size). VIX >40 = crisis (reduce all exposure). Dynamically adjust strategy allocation and leverage. CRYPTO: Use 30-day realized volatility on BTC. <40% annualized = low vol. 40-80% = medium. >80% = high. >120% = crisis. Switch between trend following (high vol) and mean reversion (low vol) strategies based on regime.

THE PARADOX OF STOPS — Adding stops to trend systems IMPROVES psychology (feels safer) but WORSENS performance (cuts winners). For MA-based systems, NO stop configuration beat no-stop on ANY metric. For breakout systems, wide stops (4+ ATR) perform nearly as well as no stops. Use stops for psychology, not optimization. CRYPTO: Due to 24/7 volatility, stops ARE necessary for survival—but use wide stops (3-4× ATR) for trend systems, not tight ones.

CONFLUENCE SCORING — Rank signals by confluence: momentum alignment + pattern completion + price target proximity + time target zone. Trade only top 20% of signals. Dramatically improves win rate and R/R. Better to miss trades than take low-quality setups. CRYPTO: Score each BTC setup 0-100. Only trade setups scoring >70. Factors: trend alignment (25%), S/R proximity (20%), momentum (20%), volume (15%), sentiment (10%), on-chain (10%).

NO-EXCUSE TRADING — Every trade must have: (1) Momentum alignment on dual timeframe. (2) Pattern showing trend or correction. (3) Price at Fibonacci S/R. (4) Time target zone reached. (5) Specific entry price and stop. (6) Position size calculated. If ANY element missing—no trade. Discipline = profits. CRYPTO: In 24/7 markets, the temptation to trade 'because I can' is enormous. This checklist prevents impulsive entries.

EMOTIONAL PROCESSING — Suppressing trading emotions causes them to sabotage decisions. When you can sit with discomfort of drawdown without flinching, you can follow your system. Emotional mastery > technical mastery. Process emotions through journaling, peer discussion, or structured review. When aware of fear or joy, use it as signal to tighten discipline. Your system was built when calm—follow it when emotional.

SYSTEM COMMITMENT — Minimum 100 trades before evaluating a system. 30-trade sample hides edge. Abandoning system during drawdown = experiencing all losses, missing all wins. Switching systems = guaranteed you'll experience every system's losses while missing its winners. CRYPTO: Commit to minimum 3 months of live trading before evaluating. Don't switch strategies based on last week's P&L.

NARRATIVE EXHAUSTION — Each BTC narrative attracts different capital. When narrative exhausts marginal buyer, price stagnates. Track: media coverage intensity, Google Trends, social media mentions. If recycling old narratives ('digital gold' again), cycle is mature. New narrative needed for new leg up. When no new narrative emerges while price stalls = distribution phase. CRYPTO: Each cycle has 2-3 dominant narratives. When they've all been exhausted, the top is near.

WHALE TRACKING — Monitor large wallet movements. BTC moving to exchanges = potential selling. To cold storage = accumulation. Large OTC trades signal institutional activity. Whale wallet clustering analysis reveals accumulation/distribution patterns. CRYPTO: Use on-chain analytics (Glassnode, Nansen, Arkham) to track whale behavior. When whales accumulate while retail sells = bullish divergence. When whales distribute while retail buys = bearish.

CRYPTO CORRELATION TRADING — When historically correlated assets diverge, trade the convergence. BTC/ETH ratio, SOL/AVAX ratio, DeFi/BTC ratio. When ratio deviates >2σ from 30-day mean, enter mean reversion. Exit at mean. CRYPTO: These pairs have structural relationships that reversion after short-term dislocations. Use 30-day rolling correlation and z-score. If correlation breaks permanently (>60 days), exit.

CALENDAR SPREAD (CRYPTO OPTIONS) — Sell near-term option, buy longer-term same strike. Profits from time decay differential and/or rising IV. Best when IV term structure is in contango (longer-dated > near-term). CRYPTO: On Deribit, sell weekly BTC options, buy monthly at same strike. Profit as near-term decays faster. Close when near-term expires. Risk: large directional move can cause losses in both legs.

BUTTERFLY SPREAD — Buy 1 ITM, sell 2 ATM, buy 1 OTM. Max profit at middle strike at expiry. Profits from decay and pinning. Defined risk = net debit. CRYPTO: On Deribit, use around max-pain price before expiry. Best in low-vol, range-bound conditions. Max profit = width between strikes - net debit. Use when expecting BTC to pin near a specific level.

BACKSPREAD — Sell 1 ATM, buy 2+ OTM. Can be credit or small debit. Unlimited upside potential. 'Free lottery' if credit. CRYPTO: Use before major events where large moves possible. Buy 2 OTM BTC calls, sell 1 ATM to finance. If BTC makes large move up, profits from 2× long delta. If stays flat, small loss or breakeven. Best when IV rank is low (cheap OTM options).

MARKET MAKER BEHAVIOR — MMs profit from bid-ask spread, hedge delta-neutral. They add liquidity at extremes, remove during trends. When MMs pull liquidity (wide spreads, thin books), volatility incoming. Track: bid-ask spread width, order book depth changes, trade size distribution. CRYPTO: MM behavior on CEXs differs from DEXs. On CEXs, MMs can withdraw instantly. On DEXs, liquidity is contract-bound. Monitor for liquidity withdrawal as volatility signal.

CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION — Don't vary position size based on confidence—confidence is unreliable. Use mechanical rules for EVERY decision. Take every signal, manage risk per trade, let edge play out over hundreds of trades. Like a casino: lose individual hands but win over thousands. Individual trades are meaningless—need 100+ for edge to manifest. Faith made $78K in training while other Turtles made $25K—same system, different execution psychology.

ANCHORING BIAS — Investors anchor to purchase price and refuse to sell at loss. Trend followers have no anchor—entry price irrelevant to exit. 'The market doesn't know what price you paid.' In crypto, anchoring to previous ATH ('BTC should be $100K because...') is narrative-driven, not valuation. MVRV, NVT, Stock-to-Flow provide pseudo-fundamental anchors. When price deviates >2σ from these, mean-reversion likely.

RECENCY BIAS — Weighting recent trades too heavily. After 5 wins, feel invincible. After 5 losses, feel defeated. Both are wrong—the next trade is independent of the last 5. Track equity curve over 100+ trades, not last 10. Like flipping a coin: getting 5 heads doesn't make tails more likely. But in trading, each trade IS independent. Don't let recent results influence your next decision.

OVERCONFIDENCE AFTER WINS — Biggest losses come after biggest wins. Overconfidence leads to oversized positions and ignored risk management. After a 10x altcoin win, your next trade is your most dangerous. Take a break. Reduce size. Euphoria of winning is more dangerous than despair of losing. Livermore made and lost fortunes—biggest losses after biggest wins. Pre-commit to reducing size after any trade >5R.

SYSTEM CONTRIBUTION — Trading system contributes ~20% to success. Position sizing ~30%. Psychology ~50%. A mediocre system with great money management will outperform great analysis with no money management. ALWAYS define risk BEFORE entry. Size positions by risk, not conviction. Focus on process, not outcome. The system itself is less important than your ability to follow it consistently.

ACCOUNT SIZE ADAPTATIONS — Small accounts (<$10K): Always trade minimum position size. Risk ≤2% per trade. Can't use optimal f—risk of ruin too high. Focus on survival. Add size only after 50%+ growth. Large accounts (>$100K): Can use half-Kelly and fractional sizing. Scale positions based on equity changes. Combined strategies across types reduce drawdown 40%+ with only 10% less return.

PLATEAU MONEY MANAGEMENT — After 10% drawdown from equity peak, reduce position size by 50%. After 20% drawdown, trade minimum size. Only increase after account returns to new equity high. Prevents catastrophic drawdowns during losing streaks while allowing full participation during winning streaks. Creates natural equity curve trending upward with controlled pullbacks. CRYPTO: Adapt thresholds—use 5%/15% instead of 10%/20% due to higher volatility.

DRAWDOWN RULES — After 3 consecutive losers: stop trading for the day, review. After 5 consecutive losers: stop for the week. After 10% account DD: reduce size 50%. After 20%: minimum size only. After 25%: close all, take 1-2 week break. Return to paper trading until 20+ profitable simulated trades. Then return at half previous size. CRYPTO: Use tighter thresholds due to faster moves. After 3 consecutive losses in 24 hours, stop for 48 hours minimum.

FLAT TIME METRIC — Longest period between new equity highs. Top CTAs: 12-18 months flat time. If backtest shows 24+ months, system may be untradeable psychologically. Solution: Combine with uncorrelated strategy to reduce flat time. Two systems with 12-month flat individually may have 6-month combined. CRYPTO: Crypto's higher volatility means shorter flat periods—but also deeper drawdowns within those periods.

MAXIMUM ADVERSE EXCURSION (MAE) — Track maximum amount each trade goes against you before hitting target. Plot MAE for all winners and losers separately. Winners rarely go beyond certain MAE threshold (e.g., 1.5 ATR). Losers regularly exceed it. Use MAE to set optimal stop: just beyond MAE threshold of winning trades. Tighter stops than arbitrary levels while keeping winners intact. CRYPTO: Calculate MAE on BTC trades to find optimal stop distance for your system.

MAXIMUM FAVORABLE EXCURSION (MFE) — Track maximum profit each trade achieves before exit. Use to set realistic profit targets. If average MFE is 3R but you're taking profit at 1R, you're leaving money on the table. E-ratio = MFE/MAE normalized by ATR. E70 > 1.0 = positive edge. 20-day Donchian breakout E70 = 1.20. With trend filter, E-ratio improves to 1.35+. CRYPTO: BTC breakout E-ratios are typically higher than traditional markets due to momentum persistence.

OPPORTUNITY FACTOR — Frequency dramatically affects returns. Same expectancy: 100 trades/day = massive annual. 100 trades/year = modest. BUT: more opportunity = more costs. Optimize: (expectancy × opportunity) / transaction costs. Don't confuse activity with profitability. Overtrading kills through costs. Undertrading leaves money on table. Find optimal frequency. CRYPTO: 24/7 market = infinite opportunity. This makes overtrading the #1 risk. Set maximum trades per day/week.

BARBELL STRATEGY — 85-90% safe (cash, stables, bonds) + 10-15% asymmetric (high-risk/high-reward crypto bets). Avoid the middle—tail risk without upside. CRYPTO: 85% in BTC/ETH/stables + 15% in high-conviction altcoin bets or deep OTM options. The 15% can generate outsized returns while the 85% ensures survival. This is how you capture crypto's upside without existential risk.

SKEWNESS AND ASYMMETRY — Crypto returns are massively negatively skewed—small gains most days, occasional catastrophic losses. Median trade return is lower than mean. Design strategies for median outcomes, not mean. Use stops to truncate left tail. Position size for the loss scenario, not the win scenario. Seek positive skew: $1 risk for $3+ reward. Asymmetric payoff mathematically superior even at 40% win rate.

TREND FOLLOWING IS 50/50 PER TRADE — Any individual trade has ~50% chance. Edge comes from asymmetric payoff—losses small (stopped out), wins large (trend riding). Need rare big winners to offset all small losses. In crypto, one 10x altcoin trend can pay for 20 stopped-out trades. Size small, let winners run. Accept 60-70% loss rate. Big winners (10-20R+) more than compensate.

CUTTING LOSSES IS PARAMOUNT — If losses get out of control, overwhelmed financially AND emotionally. In crypto's volatility, 10% loss can become 50% overnight. Set stops BEFORE entry. Never move stop further from entry. Averaging down on losing position in crypto is financial suicide. Market doesn't know your cost basis—it doesn't care. 'Losers average losers'—Paul Tudor Jones. This single rule prevents account destruction.

THE BIG MOVE — Don't scalp small profits—position for the big move. In crypto, big moves (50-200% BTC, 1000%+ alts) make the year. Most profits should come from 1-3 trades per year. Everything else is noise management. Livermore: 'Instead of placing piking bets on next few quotations, my game was to anticipate what was going to happen in a big way.' CRYPTO: Be positioned for the rare massive trend. Don't miss it by taking small profits.

FEAR PARADOX — Scariest trades often best. Panicking crowd = best risk/reward. Fear = crowd on wrong side. When everyone terrified, opportunity knocks. But don't buy panic directly—wait for tail confirmation and momentum shift. CRYPTO: Best crypto buying opportunities when on-chain shows maximum pain AND price shows reversal pattern (kangaroo tail, bullish divergence). Combine fear with technical confirmation.

INDEPENDENT THINKING — Nobody can give you a tip that will make more money than your own judgment. Following influencer calls, Telegram signals, Twitter tips makes you dependent. When tipper is wrong or absent, you have no plan. Develop your own system. Use others' ideas as research starting points, never as execution triggers. CRYPTO: The crypto influencer ecosystem is particularly toxic—most are paid promoters, not traders.

TRADE THE RIGHT SIDE — Livermore: 'It is not the bull side or the bear side, but the RIGHT side.' Trade what market is doing, not what you want. In crypto: don't be permanently bullish or bearish. Be long when trend is up, short when down, flat when unclear. Ego-attachment to direction is account suicide. Many crypto traders are 'perma-bulls' who get destroyed in bear markets. The market doesn't care about your ideology.

LIVERMORE'S PYRAMIDING — Start with small position, add as trade proves correct—NEVER adding to loser. Biggest winners were positions he kept adding to. CRYPTO: Start with 25% of intended position. Add 25% each time price confirms (breaks resistance, holds support). Maximum 100% only on strongest trends. This ensures average entry is in a winning position. Combined with ATR-based sizing, creates powerful asymmetric payoff.

RULE OF CONSERVATION — Livermore: 'A man must believe in himself and his judgment if he expects to make a living. But he must also conserve his capital.' Self-belief without capital preservation is worthless. Always maintain enough cash to take advantage of next opportunity. Being fully invested = no buying power when real opportunity arrives. CRYPTO: Keep 10-20% in stables at all times. In crypto, the best opportunities come during crashes when everyone else is forced to sell.

LEARN FROM LOSSES — Livermore: 'In losing money I have gained experience and accumulated a lot of valuable don'ts.' Went broke multiple times but never made same mistake twice. Keep detailed loss journal. After every loss: what went wrong, why, what you'll do differently. Losses are tuition. Uneducated trader pays tuition repeatedly for same lesson. CRYPTO: Every loss teaches something. Document it. The lesson is worth more than the money lost.

WAIT FOR THE RIGHT MOMENT — Livermore: 'I must not begin to advance until I am sure I shall not have to retreat.' Patience for right setup > trading frequently. In crypto's 24/7 market, there's always something happening—most is noise. Wait for A+ setups. Sitting in cash IS a position. The hardest trade is often no trade. 'When the money was not coming in, it was going out. Patience was the one quality that saved me.'

TRADE LIKE A CASINO — Dennis's method: take edges, no matter how small, with volume. End of day, edges equal profit. In crypto market-making and arbitrage: collect small edges (bid-ask spread, funding arb, basis trades) with high volume. Over many trades, small edges compound. Think like the house, not the gambler. The house doesn't worry about individual hands—only that the math works over thousands of trials.

MASTERY TAKES YEARS — Livermore: 'It took me five years to learn to play the game intelligently enough to make big money when I was right.' Trading mastery requires years of deliberate practice. No shortcuts. In crypto, 'get rich quick' gurus sell false hope. Commit to the long game. Study markets daily. Journal every trade. Expect to be unprofitable for 1-2 years minimum. The game taught me the game—learn by doing, not theory.

PREMARKET CHECKLIST — (1) Review monthly, weekly charts and MACD/stochastic direction. (2) Update squeeze status for each market/timeframe. (3) Check where market closed vs daily pivot. (4) Note key levels. (5) Review 60-min RSI divergences. (6) Check 200-period EMA as major S/R. (7) Plan for gap/no-gap scenarios. (8) Set alerts at key levels. CRYPTO: Do this at your defined 'session start' time, even though market never closes.

TRADING DAY STRUCTURE — First 2 hours: highest probability setups. Midday (11:30-2:00 PM ET): low probability chop. Final 2 hours: trend resumption or end-of-day plays. NEVER initiate after 3:30 PM. CRYPTO: Adapt to your timezone. Since 24/7, identify the highest-volume periods for your traded assets. BTC volume peaks during US/EU overlap (13:00-17:00 UTC). Trade during high-liquidity windows, avoid low-liquidity periods.

R-MULTIPLES — R = initial risk on a trade (entry to initial stop). All trades measured as multiples of R. +3R winner, -1R loser, -0.5R partial stop. Track distribution. Healthy: average winner >> average loser. Look for occasional huge winners (10R+) compensating for many small losers. Removes size bias—judge 100+ trades. CRYPTO: Track R-multiples on every trade. Your goal is not to maximize win rate but to maximize average R per trade.

SNOW FIGHT METAPHOR — Each trade = snowball thrown at wall. White = profits, black = losses. Net effect = equity change. THREE variables: (1) Reliability (white vs black probability). (2) Reward-to-risk (size of white vs black). (3) Opportunity (how many snowballs). Multiply all three = expectancy. Position sizing = how many walls you're building simultaneously. All three matter, not just reliability.

TIGHT VS WIDE STOPS — Tight: smaller losses, multiple re-entries, bigger R-multiples. BUT: decrease reliability, increase costs, miss moves. Wide: fewer whipsaws, better reliability, lower costs. BUT: bigger losses when wrong. Optimal: use MAE analysis to find minimum effective stop distance. Don't use arbitrary numbers. CRYPTO: Due to 24/7 volatility, 'tight' in crypto = 2× ATR. 'Wide' = 4× ATR. Never use fixed-dollar stops—always volatility-adjusted.

TRANSACTION COST IMPACT — Rarely see system producing profits much bigger than its costs. If $1M net profit, probably >$1M in costs. Factor: commissions, slippage, bid-ask, market impact. Target: costs <50% of gross profit. CRYPTO: Exchange fees 0.04-0.1%, slippage 0.05-0.5%, funding rates variable, withdrawal fees. High-frequency crypto strategies need extremely tight execution. One round-trip on BTC perp = ~0.1% in fees alone.

SYSTEM DETERIORATION MONITORING — Track expectancy monthly. If drops below zero for 3 consecutive months, investigate. Causes: (1) Market regime change. (2) Overfit to past data. (3) Random losing streak. (4) Execution errors. Response: reduce size 50%, analyze recent trades, paper trade to verify, consider shelving. Professional system traders constantly monitor and adapt. All mechanical systems eventually self-destruct—monitor and evolve.

POSITION SIZING MODEL COMPARISON — (1) Fixed units per $X: worst model, ignores volatility. (2) Equal value: divide capital equally, see leverage clearly. (3) Percent risk: most popular, 0.5-1% per trade. (4) Percent volatility: equalizes risk across instruments, most sophisticated. Same system, 5 models on Dow stocks over 5.5 years: No sizing 1.75% annual. Equal leverage 8.66%. 1% risk 20.92%. 0.5% volatility 17.52%. Position sizing DRAMATICALLY changes results.

THE HOLY GRAIL IS YOU — Most look for mysterious system. Real Holy Grail: understanding yourself—biases, objectives, risk tolerance, time availability, emotional triggers. Different people need different systems based on personality. System that works for one may fail for another due to psychological compatibility. Self-knowledge comes BEFORE system design. Write objectives first. System that doesn't match objectives will fail even if technically profitable.

12 STEPS TO SYSTEM DEVELOPMENT — (1) Take inventory (objectives, capital, time). (2) Develop open mind. (3) Determine objectives. (4) Choose timeframe. (5) Study best historical moves. (6) Identify concept behind moves. (7) Add stops and transaction costs. (8) Add profit exits, calculate expectancy. (9) Look for huge reward trades. (10) Optimize with position sizing. (11) Determine improvements. (12) Worst-case scenario mental planning.

CAN SLIM (ADAPTED FOR CRYPTO) — C=Current quarterly metrics (TVL growth, revenue, users). A=Annual fundamentals (network growth, developer activity). N=New (new protocols, upgrades, narratives). S=Supply (token unlocks, emission schedule). L=Leader (relative strength vs sector). I=Institutional (VC backing, fund holdings). M=Market direction (BTC trend). Concentrate 5-8 leaders. Exit if below 50-day MA on heavy volume.

8% STOP RULE (O'NEIL) — Cut losses 8% below purchase price. No exceptions. If you buy 10 stocks, cut 7 at small losses, you need 2-3 at 100-200%+ to compensate. CRYPTO: Adapt to 10-15% stop for BTC (higher volatility), 15-20% for alts. The principle remains: define maximum loss BEFORE entry and honor it absolutely. Never rationalize holding past your stop. The market doesn't care about your thesis.

MARKET DIRECTION SIGNALS — 3-5 distribution days in 2-3 weeks = top forming. Follow-through day: 1.5%+ gain on heavier volume after market bottom = confirmed uptrend. CRYPTO: Adapt using BTC breadth—track how many top-50 alts are making new 30-day highs vs lows. When NH-NL turns negative while BTC makes new highs = internal weakness, distribution. When NH-NL turns positive during BTC lows = accumulation, reversal signal.

ETF/CRYPTO ROTATION MODEL — Rank sectors by 3-month relative strength monthly. Long top 3. Rebalance monthly. Outperformed 3-5% annually in traditional markets. CRYPTO: Rank crypto sectors (L1s, DeFi, memes, infrastructure, gaming) by 90-day RS. Long top 3 sectors. Rebalance monthly. Max 30% per sector. Use sector indices where available. This simple momentum rotation captures sector-level trends without individual coin selection risk.

NH-NL INDEX FOR CRYPTO — New High-New Low Index: how many stocks making new highs vs lows. Adapted: track how many top-50 alts making new 30-day highs vs lows. When NH-NL turns negative while BTC makes new highs = internal weakness, distribution phase. When NH-NL turns positive during BTC lows = accumulation, reversal signal. Use as confirmation of trend quality—strong trends have broad participation.
